recipes.title: Köttbullar
recipes.description: Swedish meatballs of beef and pork in a creamy gravy, served with lingonberry jam and mashed potato.
steps: Soak breadcrumbs in milk 5 minutes.
Sauté finely chopped onion in 20g butter until soft; cool.
Combine meats, soaked breadcrumbs, onion, egg, allspice and salt; knead well.
Roll into walnut-size meatballs.
Brown in remaining butter in batches; remove.
Whisk flour into pan drippings, then add stock, cream and soy; simmer until thick.
Return meatballs and warm through 5 minutes.
Serve with mashed potato and lingonberry jam.

recipes.title: Gravlax
recipes.description: Swedish cured salmon with dill, sugar and salt, sliced thin and served with mustard sauce.
steps: Crush peppercorns coarsely and mix with salt and sugar.
Lay salmon skin-side down on a tray and rub with cure.
Cover with chopped dill, pressing in firmly.
Wrap tightly in cling film, weight with a plate, and refrigerate 48 hours, turning once.
Whisk mustard, honey and vinegar; slowly drizzle in oil to emulsify, then stir in chopped dill.
Scrape cure off salmon, pat dry, and slice thinly across the grain.
Serve with mustard-dill sauce, rye bread and lemon.

recipes.title: Pyttipanna
recipes.description: Swedish hash of diced potato, onion and meat fried golden, topped with a fried egg and pickled beets.
steps: Dice potatoes, meat and onion into 1cm cubes.
Heat half the butter and oil; fry potatoes until deep golden.
Push potatoes aside, add remaining fat and fry meat until crisp.
Add onion and cook 5 minutes until soft.
Toss everything together, season and keep warm.
Fry eggs sunny-side up in butter.
Top each portion of hash with an egg and serve with pickled beets.

recipes.title: Janssons Frestelse
recipes.description: Swedish gratin of potato matchsticks, onion, anchovies and cream baked until golden.
steps: Cut potatoes into thin matchsticks; rinse and pat dry.
Slice onions thin and sauté in 30g butter until soft and golden.
Butter a baking dish and layer half the potatoes, all the onions and anchovies.
Top with remaining potatoes; pepper generously.
Pour cream and milk over (plus a few spoons of anchovy brine).
Scatter breadcrumbs and dot with remaining butter.
Bake at 200°C for 50 minutes until tender and golden on top.

recipes.title: Smörgåstårta
recipes.description: Swedish savoury layered sandwich cake with bread, mayo, shrimp, smoked salmon and herbs.
steps: Trim crusts from bread; arrange 8 slices in a rectangle on a serving board.
Beat cream cheese with mayonnaise and sour cream until smooth; reserve a third.
Spread a layer of cream cheese mix; top with chopped eggs and half the dill.
Add a second bread layer; spread cream cheese, then layer salmon and lemon juice.
Add a third bread layer; press gently and chill 30 minutes.
Coat the entire cake with reserved cream cheese mix like icing.
Decorate top with shrimp, cucumber slices and dill sprigs.
Chill at least 4 hours before slicing into rectangles.

recipes.title: Kanelbullar
recipes.description: Swedish cinnamon buns with cardamom dough, swirled with butter and pearl sugar.
steps: Warm milk to 37°C; whisk in yeast and rest 10 minutes.
Add sugar, cardamom, salt, melted butter, egg and most of the flour; knead 8 minutes.
Cover and rise 1 hour until doubled.
Mix soft butter, brown sugar and cinnamon for filling.
Roll dough into a 40x30cm rectangle, spread filling and roll up tightly.
Cut into 14 slices and place on a lined tray; cover and rise 45 minutes.
Brush with beaten egg and sprinkle with pearl sugar.
Bake at 200°C for 12 minutes until golden; cool on a rack.

recipes.title: Räksmörgås
recipes.description: Iconic Swedish open-faced shrimp sandwich on rye with egg, mayo, dill and lemon.
steps: Lightly butter the rye bread.
Layer a lettuce leaf on each slice.
Slice eggs and arrange three slices per sandwich.
Pile shrimp generously on top of eggs.
Spoon mayonnaise over the shrimp.
Top with chopped dill, a grind of pepper and a lemon wedge.

recipes.title: Ärtsoppa
recipes.description: Traditional Swedish yellow pea soup with smoked pork, served with mustard.
steps: Soak split peas overnight in cold water; drain.
Place pork knuckle in a large pot with 2.5L water; bring to simmer and skim.
Add peas, chopped onions, carrots, bay, thyme and marjoram.
Simmer covered 90 minutes until pork is tender.
Remove pork, shred meat and return to pot.
Discard bay; adjust salt.
Serve hot with a dollop of mustard.

recipes.title: Kåldolmar
recipes.description: Swedish cabbage rolls stuffed with rice and pork, served with cream gravy and lingonberry.
steps: Core cabbage and blanch whole 5 minutes; peel softened leaves.
Simmer rice in milk until creamy and absorbed; cool.
Sauté chopped onion in butter; cool.
Combine meats, rice, onion, egg, allspice and salt; mix well.
Place 2 tablespoons filling on each leaf, fold and roll tightly.
Arrange seam-down in a baking dish, brush with syrup, dot with butter.
Pour stock over and bake at 200°C for 60 minutes, basting twice.
Pour pan juices into a saucepan, add cream, simmer to gravy and serve over rolls.

recipes.title: Falukorv
recipes.description: Swedish baked sausage glazed with mustard, served with macaroni or potatoes.
steps: Score the falukorv on the diagonal across the top.
Whisk mustard, ketchup and honey to a glaze.
Brush sausage all over with the glaze.
Place in a baking dish, dot with butter, bake at 200°C for 25 minutes.
Boil macaroni in salted water until al dente; drain.
Slice the falukorv thickly and serve with the macaroni.

recipes.title: Princess Cake
recipes.description: Swedish layered sponge cake with raspberry, vanilla cream and whipped cream, covered in green marzipan.
steps: Whisk eggs and sugar 8 minutes until tripled; fold in sifted flour, starch and baking powder.
Bake in a 24cm tin at 175°C for 25 minutes; cool and slice into three layers.
Whisk yolks, sugar, cornflour and a little milk; warm the rest of the milk.
Combine and cook, whisking, until thick; stir in vanilla and chill the pastry cream.
Whip cream to firm peaks.
Place bottom sponge on a plate, spread jam, then a layer of pastry cream.
Add the middle sponge, spread more cream, then the top sponge.
Cover the cake in a smooth dome of whipped cream.
Roll marzipan thinly and drape over the cake; trim and dust with icing sugar.

recipes.title: Kroppkakor
recipes.description: Swedish potato dumplings stuffed with smoked pork, served with butter and lingonberry.
steps: Grate raw potatoes finely and squeeze out all liquid.
Mash boiled potatoes; combine with raw potato, flour, yolks and salt.
Dice pork and onion; sauté until onion is soft and pork crisp; add allspice.
Take a handful of potato dough, flatten on your palm, place a spoon of pork filling in the centre.
Close dough around filling and roll into a smooth ball; flour lightly.
Drop into boiling salted water and simmer 15 minutes once they float.
Drain and serve hot with melted butter and lingonberry jam.
